text: The surgeon creates a transverse incision in the peritoneum from the anterior superior iliac spine towards the medial umbilical ligament. The preperitoneal space is entered, and a preperitoneal flap is created. Dissection continues medially towards the hernia defect, revealing significant scarring from the previous repair. Sutures from the prior surgery are visible. Using risted robotic instruments, the surgeon performs precise dissection despite the scarring. A three-dimensional mesh is placed into the pre-peritoneal space to cover the defect, ensuring appropriate coverage to prevent future recurrence.

The surgeon secures the mesh medially to Cooper's ligaments using 2-0 ethibond sutures in a simple interrupted fashion. The remaining mesh is affixed to the abdominal wall with surgical glue. The peritoneal defect is closed primarily with absorbable sutures in a simple running fashion.
The surgeon includes the hernia sac and preperitoneal fat in the closure to reinforce the peritoneal flap, completing the repair of the recurrent right inguinal hernia.
